    Type to search · ↑↓ to move · Enter to open · Esc to close

    Home › Records › Longest flights

    The longest flights in the world.

    Every scheduled route over 12,900 km, ranked by great-circle distance. The current record is Singapore to New York — and Qantas has already announced the flight that will beat it by 1,700 km, sometimes routing directly over the North Pole.

    Distances are great-circle figures. Compiled 2026-07-14 from: Longest flights — Wikipedia (CC BY-SA), July 2026 · OAG — the world's longest routes by great-circle distance · Qantas Project Sunrise announcement, June 2026. Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    More boards

    Other records

    The 50 longest aircraft ever built

    Ranked by fuselage length — the definitive board, from the An-225 down to the Il-76.

    Open the board →

    The heaviest aircraft ever built

    Ranked by maximum take-off weight — the total mass an aircraft is certified to lift off the runway.

    Open the board →

    The fastest aircraft here

    Ranked by maximum speed — where the supersonics leave everything else standing.

    Open the board →

    The most-produced large aircraft

    Ranked by how many were actually built — where the giants of the size charts meet the giants of the sales charts.

    Open the board →

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
